title: The Global Economic Landscape
summary:
Main
By Michalis Voutsinas
The global economic landscape has undergone a profound transformation over the past five years, shaped by a succession of disruptive forces that have altered market behaviour, redefined policy priorities, and recalibrated the trajectory of international trade. For the maritime sector, particularly the dry bulk market, these developments have not merely represented cyclical fluctuations but structural shifts whose influence continues to reverberate through freight dynamics, investment decisions, and commodity flows. The Covid-19 pandemic stands as the first and perhaps most destabilising rupture in this sequence.
